original_text: Airbnb faces material financial volatility risk, as evidenced by significant quarter-to-quarter fluctuations in both revenue and profitability. Revenue declined 39.12% YoY in Q1 2025 before rebounding with 36.27% YoY growth in Q2 2025 (K001). Net income exhibited even more pronounced volatility, falling 88.74% YoY in Q1 2025 to $154M (C014, K002), then surging 316.88% YoY in Q2 2025 to $642M (C007, K002). This volatility suggests exposure to seasonal travel demand patterns and macroeconomic sensitivity, which could complicate financial planning and investor confidence. Severity: High.

Net income exhibited even more pronounced volatility, falling 88.74% YoY in Q1 2025 to $154M (C014, K002), then surging 316.88% YoY in Q2 2025 to $642M (C007, K002). This volatility suggests exposure to seasonal travel demand patterns and macroeconomic sensitivity, which could complicate financial planning and investor confidence. Severity: High.
changes_summary: Added revenue chunks (C011, C004) to support revenue data claims, addressing the critical issue of missing revenue evidence.
